_comment: Content-flag retry e2e (#881). Two sequential responses keyed on model + sequenceIndex (no userMessage, so it's decoupled from any LLM-recorded prompt). seedream_v5 is used ONLY by the content-retry spec, so these never interfere with other specs. Attempt 0 returns a 422 content-checker rejection; the SAME-model retry (attempt 1) succeeds. The fal client does NOT retry 422s (retryable codes are 429/502/503/504), so the two attempts consume sequenceIndex 0 then 1 exactly. The rejection MESSAGE is the verbatim string the real provider returns (captured 2026-06-11 from openai/gpt-image-2 via fal). NOTE: real fal wraps it as { detail: [{ msg, type }] }, but aimock's error fixtures can only emit the OpenAI-style { error: { message } } shape, so that's what's used here; extractFalErrorMessage reads both (body.detail for prod, body.error.message here) and isContentRejectionError matches 'content checker'. The real { detail } shape is pinned in src/lib/ai/fal-error.test.ts + content-rejection.test.ts.

error.message: The content could not be processed because it contained material flagged by a content checker.
